core_mechanism: diagnosing chaos vs. integrability by testing whether a system's stationary wave modes conform statistically to a Gaussian random superposition of plane waves (a random-wave / maximum-entropy null field), cross-checked against the classical phase-space picture via Poincare-section structure and the fraction of surviving invariant (KAM) tori
brief_reason: The abstract diagnoses wave chaos through compliance with the Berry random-wave hypothesis and diagnoses the underlying ray/geodesic dynamics through Poincare sections and the measure of KAM tori, contrasting a random-field statistical signature of chaos with the persistence of regular invariant tori.
